message:
perf(pager): O(1) AtomicPublishedPages::remove via slot back-pointer

AtomicPublishedPages::remove was scanning `active_indices` with
`iter().position(|&i| i == idx)` — O(n) in the number of currently
live direct-slot pages — before calling `swap_remove(pos)`. Under
MT-writer contention, publication rollback/retry exercises remove
repeatedly and `active_indices` can grow to thousands of entries
before being drained, making the linear scan the dominant cost on
the remove hot path.

Store the position of each live slot inside the slot itself as
`active_pos: AtomicUsize`. On insert we record the tail index before
pushing to `active_indices`; on remove we load the stored position
and do a single O(1) `swap_remove`, then refresh the back-pointer of
the element that moved into the vacated slot (if any). `retain`
rebuilds the vec and refreshes back-pointers for the surviving
indices, so the O(1) invariant holds across retain cycles too.

The `active_pos` field lives alongside `present`/`page` inside
`AtomicPublishedPageSlot` and is only read/written while the
`active_indices` mutex is held, so `Relaxed` ordering is sufficient.
A defensive `iter().position()` fallback remains for the (unreachable
in practice) case where the stored back-pointer is stale — structure
stays consistent even if that guard ever trips rather than leaving a
dangling active-index entry.

Before: O(n) linear scan per remove — 1.050377 ms / 4000 removes
                                     = ~263 ns/remove (plain Vec ops only)
After:  O(1) back-pointer per remove — 85.38 µs / 4000 removes
                                     = ~21 ns/remove (including slot mutex + page.take())
Delta:  ~12.3x faster on the remove hot path, despite the O(1) path
        also doing the slot mutex lock + page.take() that the
        reference scan skips — the underlying algorithmic win is
        even larger.
Bench:  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_1777000277 \
        cargo test -p fsqlite-pager atomic_published_pages -- --nocapture
        (test `test_atomic_published_pages_remove_is_o1_vs_linear_scan`)
Bead:   bd-dgpfx

Verification:
- cargo check -p fsqlite-pager (via rch) — pass
- cargo test -p fsqlite-pager published_pages (8/8 pass incl.
  new `remove_middle_and_tail_are_consistent` across head/middle/tail
  removal orderings)
- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager --all-targets -- -D warnings — clean
- cargo fmt --check — clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
